findings.finding: Test-file docstring line 15 declares check_tier as kw-only (check_tier(*, agent_name, model, tier="free")), but the implementation and the Sage decisions doc §3 show it as positional-or-keyword. No test actually enforces kw-only (no TypeError-on-positional assertion), and every call site uses keyword form, so behavior is safe. The drift is doc-only.
findings.recommendation: Align the test docstring to the spec and impl (drop the leading *) OR add the * in impl and a TypeError assertion. Non-blocking for merge.

findings.finding: On exception-only exhaustion the final envelope is rebuilt from envelope.with_error(ErrorDetail(error_type='infrastructure', message=str(exc))). If an earlier attempt produced a FAILED envelope with useful ErrorDetail (retryable non-terminal), that detail is replaced with the last str(exc). V1-verbatim and Sage §6 lock this, so behavior is correct-by-spec, but operators debugging mixed failure modes will see only the last exception message.
findings.recommendation: Accept for v0.1 (spec-locked). Consider a follow-up ticket to preserve last non-infrastructure error_type if it exists, for richer telemetry.

findings.finding: _emit() does not catch exceptions from bus.emit() itself. bus.emit() (events/bus.py:89-129) isolates HANDLER failures but the emit call path itself (sequence increment, model_copy) is not broken today. Any future bus.emit()-raising bug would propagate into the except-Exception retry handler at line 201 and be misattributed as an infrastructure failure, incrementing retries. Not a bug today; a coupling observation.
findings.recommendation: No action. If the bus contract changes in the future, reconsider wrapping _emit with try/except.

findings.finding: Verified: delay = retry_delay * (2 ** attempt); no sleep before first attempt (sleep happens after a failed attempt, inside the attempt<max_retries guard); no sleep after final exhausted attempt (the last loop iteration's retry-block is guarded by attempt<max_retries so it is skipped); retries field counts retries not attempts (first-attempt success = 0; max_retries=3 exhaustion = 3); total call count on exhaustion = max_retries + 1. All three cardinality variants are covered by TestAttemptCardinality. Backoff determinism covered in TestBackoff. Terminal error set matches Sage §3.1 exactly (5 entries, axiom_load dropped per the public-repo adaptation).
findings.recommendation: APPROVE. Retry algorithm is correct and spec-aligned.

findings.finding: Verified: _attempt_once awaits backend.execute() directly under asyncio.timeout(); no sync-in-async mixing. SDK backend correctly uses `async with aclosing(stream)` around the async generator returned by query(), ensuring aclose() on early return (RateLimitError rejected path). time.monotonic() used for durations (not wall clock). await/emit pattern is clean.

DispatchResult uses model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True), bare-type field annotations (envelope: Envelope, cost_usd: float, etc.) — idiomatic Pydantic v2. Envelope is runtime-imported with noqa: TC001 because Pydantic needs the concrete class at model-creation time; that noqa is correct and matches the project's 'TC003 breaks Pydantic' rule. No Field(...) metadata is required because all fields are required-by-position and have trivial validation. Good.

Verified: outer try/except in execute() wraps _do_execute(); ErrorDetail populated with error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc), traceback=tb_module.format_exc(). Never-raises guarantee holds. Inner _do_execute handles RateLimitError rejection by returning an envelope-with-error (not raising), and AgentError (is_error=True from ResultMessage) similarly returned as error-envelope. Clean separation of provider-specific signals from infrastructure exceptions.

PydanticAIBackend: module-level `Agent: Any = None` sentinel, lazy import inside execute() via `global Agent`, cached after first use; importable without pydantic_ai installed (Sage §1.1). Test docstring explicitly notes this backend does NOT try/except — the runner's exception-loop is the safety net, by design. TierGate is genuinely stateless: no __init__ state, method returns True for all inputs, matrix of unicode/empty/whitespace/long-string inputs all pass.

recommendation: MERGE. Implementation is V1-verbatim, async patterns are sound, retry algorithm is correct (spec-aligned on all three edges: no pre-sleep, no post-exhaustion sleep, retries-counts-retries), SDK backend error wrapping is complete with traceback capture, lazy import pattern for pydantic_ai is clean, TierGate is stateless. 639/639 tests green under PYTHONPATH to review worktree; ruff clean. Only one low-severity doc drift (ENG-01, check_tier test docstring claims kw-only that impl does not enforce) and one observability note (ENG-02, exception-exhaustion error_type overwrite) — both non-blocking and consistent with V1.
